title: Abdominal pain follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists sudden or severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigid abdomen, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy, and shock-associated features.
red_flags.rationale: The exact section supports clinician-assessed severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigidity, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy and shock context plus examination without auto-escalation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists sudden or severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigid abdomen, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy, and shock-associated features.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11

red_flags.text: Lists specialist and emergency referral criteria for adult abdominal pain.
red_flags.rationale: The exact section supports documenting clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up and questions without generating referral or urgency.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists specialist and emergency referral criteria for adult abdominal pain.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11

investigations.text: Specifies onset, duration, location, character, severity, associated gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma, and medical-history domains.
investigations.rationale: The exact UAE section supports interval onset, location, character, severity, change, gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma and medical history without assigning cause.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies onset, duration, location, character, severity, associated gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma, and medical-history domains.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11

investigations.text: Lists blood tests, urinalysis, pregnancy testing, pancreatic enzymes, and imaging selected according to the clinical assessment.
investigations.rationale: The exact section supports clinician review of already ordered laboratory, stool, liver, endoscopy or imaging results without ordering or interpreting them automatically.
investigations.evidence_extract: Lists blood tests, urinalysis, pregnancy testing, pancreatic enzymes, and imaging selected according to the clinical assessment.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Abdominal Pain in Adults – 11

source_limitations: Current examination, exact result interpretation, diagnosis, treatment response, medicine, pregnancy, referral and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
